name: Parallel Test Execution with Sharding
description: Scale Playwright test suites by splitting tests across multiple CI machines using sharding for dramatically faster feedback loops.
prerequisites: Playwright test suite with many tests
GitHub Actions or similar CI with matrix support

steps.title: Run tests with sharding locally
steps.description: Split test files across multiple shards for parallel execution.
steps.tip: Sharding splits test files, not individual tests. Use --shard=N/M where N is the shard index (1-based) and M is the total number of shards.

steps.title: Configure CI matrix for sharding
steps.description: Set up GitHub Actions to run shards in parallel across machines.
steps.command:
cat > .github/workflows/playwright-sharded.yml << 'EOF'
name: Playwright Sharded
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        shard: [1/4, 2/4, 3/4, 4/4]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
      - run: npx playwright test --shard=${{ matrix.shard }} --project=chromium
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
        with:
          name: blob-report-${{ strategy.job-index }}
          path: blob-report/
  merge-reports:
    if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: all-blob-reports
          pattern: blob-report-*
          merge-multiple: true
      - run: npx playwright merge-reports --reporter html ./all-blob-reports
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: playwright-report
          path: playwright-report/
EOF

steps.title: Configure blob reporter for shard merging
steps.description: Use the blob reporter to collect results from each shard for merging.

steps.title: Configure parallel workers per shard
steps.description: Tune the number of parallel workers within each shard.
steps.command:
cat << 'EOF'
// playwright.config.ts
fullyParallel: true,
workers: process.env.CI ? 2 : undefined,
EOF
steps.tip: Each shard runs its own set of workers. With 4 shards and 2 workers each, you get 8 parallel test executions across your CI cluster.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Playwright Sharding Test
set -euo pipefail

TOTAL_SHARDS=${1:-4}

echo "=== Playwright Sharding Test ==="
echo "Total shards: $TOTAL_SHARDS"
echo ""

# Estimate test distribution
TOTAL_TESTS=$(npx playwright test --list 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'test' || echo "unknown")
echo "Total tests: $TOTAL_TESTS"

echo ""
echo "=== Running Each Shard ==="
for i in $(seq 1 "$TOTAL_SHARDS"); do
  echo ""
  echo "--- Shard $i/$TOTAL_SHARDS ---"
  TESTS_IN_SHARD=$(npx playwright test --shard="$i/$TOTAL_SHARDS" --list 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'test' || echo "?")
  echo "Tests in shard: $TESTS_IN_SHARD"
done

echo ""
echo "=== CI Matrix Configuration ==="
echo "Add to your workflow:"
echo "  strategy:"
echo "    matrix:"
echo -n "      shard: ["
for i in $(seq 1 "$TOTAL_SHARDS"); do
  [ $i -gt 1 ] && echo -n ", "
  echo -n ""$i/$TOTAL_SHARDS""
done
echo "]"

faq.question: How does Playwright distribute tests across shards?
faq.answer: Playwright splits test files (not individual tests) across shards using a round-robin distribution. Each shard gets an approximately equal number of test files. Tests within a file always run in the same shard.

faq.question: How do I merge reports from multiple shards?
faq.answer: Use the blob reporter in each shard to create binary report files. After all shards complete, run 'npx playwright merge-reports --reporter html ./all-blob-reports' to combine them into a single HTML report.

faq.question: What is the optimal number of shards?
faq.answer: Start with 2-4 shards and measure. More shards add CI overhead (checkout, install, browser download). The sweet spot is where each shard runs for 2-5 minutes. For very large suites, 8-16 shards is common.
